items.title: Decide scaler inclusion from the target algorithm
items.avoid: Copy-pasting a StandardScaler step into every FE template including tree baselines.
items.prefer: Include a scale branch for k-NN, k-means, Ridge/Lasso, SVM, PCA, and NNs; omit for tree-only paths on engineered numerics.
items.why: Algorithm sensitivity determines whether magnitude matters; trees split on order, distance models do not.
items.example: ColumnTransformer numeric branch for RandomForest: imputer only; same branch for KNeighborsClassifier: imputer → StandardScaler.

items.title: Keep scaling on the numeric branch only
items.avoid: One Pipeline step that StandardScales the entire hstack of numerics and one-hot columns.
items.prefer: Separate numeric and categorical branches; scaler after imputation on continuous engineered columns.
items.why: One-hot indicators should stay 0/1; scaling them adds no benefit and harms interpretability.

items.title: Link to canonical scaler docs for formula choices
items.avoid: Re-deriving StandardScaler vs MinMax tradeoffs in every FE notebook.
items.prefer: Consult scaling and normalization for formula details; choose StandardScaler by default for distance/penalty models, MinMax when bounded inputs are required.
items.why: Single canonical owner prevents contradictory definitions across topics.
items.example: Team wiki links to data-preprocessing--scaling-and-normalization for MinMax outlier sensitivity before picking scaler for `log_revenue`.

items.title: Serialize the full pipeline after engineering stabilizes
items.avoid: Saving the estimator while scaler fit lives in an earlier notebook cell.
items.prefer: joblib.dump the fitted Pipeline containing ColumnTransformer + model once engineered column list is frozen.
items.why: Train–serve parity requires the same transforms on engineered features at inference.
items.example: joblib.dump(fitted_pipe, 'churn_knn_v3.joblib') where fitted_pipe includes the train-fitted StandardScaler on `sessions_per_week`.
